content:
# Fixed LUT + HSL Qualifier POC — Per-Object Hue Analysis (2026-04-01)

## Fixes Applied
1. σ=0 (no Gaussian smoothing) ✓
2. Nearest-neighbor fill for empty cells ✓
3. Removed ambiance compensation (was harmful) ✓
4. Added HSL qualifier correction (6-vector, DaVinci-style) ✓

## Global Metrics (avg delta vs full)
- R/G: -0.007 (close)
- Sat: -0.004 (very close — best ever)
- Hue: +0.3° average (best ever!)
- Frame 02 hue: 138.4 vs 138.9 — nearly perfect

## HSL Qualifier Corrections Derived
| Qualifier | H offset | S ratio | Pixels |
|-----------|----------|---------|--------|
| Blue | -0.96° | 1.105 | 1.09M (dominant) |
| Cyan | -2.91° | 1.066 | 125K |
| Red/Skin | -1.25° | 1.236 | 99K |
| Yellow | +4.41° | 1.194 | 75K |
| Magenta | +0.81° | 1.419 | 41K |
| Green | +4.28° | 1.579 | 3K |

Yellow qualifier has the largest hue shift (+4.41°) — this is the yellow line the user noticed.

## Per-Object Hue Analysis — THE KEY FINDING

| Frame | Object | Hue(hybrid) | Hue(full) | Δ Hue | Δ Sat |
|-------|--------|-------------|-----------|-------|-------|
| **01** | bg water | 206.0 | 205.6 | **-0.3°** | -0.020 |
| **01** | floor/reef | 197.5 | 182.9 | **+6.7°** | +0.012 |
| **01** | diver | 154.1 | 105.6 | **+10.2°** | +0.066 |
| **02** | bg water | 205.9 | 209.7 | **+3.8°** | -0.007 |
| **02** | floor/reef | 227.5 | 229.9 | **+4.8°** | +0.003 |
| **02** | diver | 137.3 | 133.7 | **+3.1°** | -0.000 |
| **03** | bg water | 205.1 | 207.3 | **+2.3°** | -0.001 |
| **03** | floor/reef | 219.1 | 216.2 | **-11.5°** | +0.068 |
| **03** | diver | 98.5 | 140.0 | **-9.6°** | -0.033 |

## Interpretation
- **Background water**: Excellent — 0.3-3.8° shift. HSL blue qualifier handles this well.
- **Floor/reef**: Variable — 4.8° to 11.5°. The reef has complex mixed colors that don't fit one HSL qualifier.
- **Diver**: Frame 02 is great (3.1°) but frames 01 and 03 have 10° shifts. The diver occupies a hue range (skin tones, equipment) that the LUT struggles with due to spatial averaging.

## Key Insight
The LUT works well for uniform color regions (water background) but fails for complex objects (divers, reef) where RAUNE-Net's spatial attention gives different corrections to neighboring pixels. The HSL qualifier helps globally but can't fix per-pixel spatial variation.

The diver hue error on frame 01 (+10.2°) vs frame 02 (+3.1°) shows the error is content-dependent, not systematic — it depends on how well the keyframe's diver colors are represented in the LUT.
